steps.description:
Click the "Create" button for the "AI Foundry Agent to M365 Copilot" sample in the "Samples" tab of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension within Visual Studio Code.
And input ${{var:app_name:tab-with-backend}} into the run time variable.
Right-click on the background area of the Explorer panel in Visual Studio Code, below the list of project files and folders, to open the context menu for the Explorer view.
Click the "Open in Integrated Terminal" option in the right-click context menu within Visual Studio Code’s Explorer panel.
Type 'func' into the integrated terminal input in Visual Studio Code within the 'hello-world-tab-with-backend' workspace.
Press Enter in the integrated terminal of Visual Studio Code to execute the partially typed "func" command in the hello-world-tab-with-backend workspace.

Click the text input area in the integrated bash terminal at the bottom of the Visual Studio Code window, positioning the cursor for command entry in the "hello-world-tab-with-backend" project workspace.
Click the "New Terminal" (+) button in the integrated terminal panel of Visual Studio Code to open a new terminal instance.
Type 'func --version' into the integrated bash terminal at the bottom of the Visual Studio Code window.
Press the Enter key in the integrated terminal of Visual Studio Code, within the "hello-world-tab-with-backend" workspace, to execute the partially typed command "func --vers".
Click inside the integrated terminal input field in Visual Studio Code to activate the cursor for command entry.
